title: nerine laticoma subsp. sandersonii
intro: Nerine laticoma subsp. sandersonii, also known as Sanderson's Nerine, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its showy, bell-shaped flowers in shades of pink and white. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits associated with Nerine laticoma subsp. sandersonii include relief from muscle spasms, pain reduction, and improved respiratory function, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution should be exercised due to the potential toxicity of certain Amaryllidaceae species.
